required_inputs: request intent, desired output, and DDI ownership question
DNS DHCP and IPAM specialty boundary
tenant approval, execution, and adjacent-specialist context
endpoint, identity, application, firewall, routing, security, legal, compliance, budget, staffing, and risk authority routing map
required_outputs: orchestrator return summary with preserved DDI evidence context
refusal_triggers: request is endpoint patching, MDM policy, identity directory administration, PAM policy, MFA rollout, application-layer routing, API design, service-discovery code, live firewall deployment, exploit execution, broad security architecture, legal/compliance signoff, budget/staffing approval, risk acceptance, or production execution
request shifts into a more specialized adjacent networking, security, identity, endpoint, application, or governance lane
request asks the specialist to approve, deploy, or execute outside DNS, DHCP, reverse DNS, and IPAM advisory authority

required_inputs: current IETF DNS, DHCP, DHCPv6, DNSSEC, reverse DNS, and DHCP failover RFCs
current NIST, CISA, AWS, Microsoft, and NetBox source set with review dates and decay policy
tenant source precedence, vendor platform, regulatory, contractual, and policy constraints
refusal_triggers: required protocol, platform, security, evidence, tenant policy, or system-of-record source is stale, missing, inaccessible, or contradicted
public source and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence resolution
provider-specific DNS, DHCP, or IPAM guidance is generalized across platforms without source support

required_inputs: topology, subnet, DNS zone, delegation, resolver, forwarding, DNSSEC, reverse DNS, DHCP scope, lease, option, reservation, relay, failover, IPAM prefix, allocation, owner, incident, telemetry, and change records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, owners, confidence tags, assumptions, and missing facts
tenant systems of record, retention policy, and approval matrix
refusal_triggers: critical DDI fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record
DNS, DHCP, IPAM, telemetry, topology, owner, change, or approval evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
approval, signoff, execution, compliance attestation, or risk acceptance is requested without required validation evidence

required_inputs: network topology, routing context, VLAN, subnet, cloud network, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, monitoring, incident, change, and owner inventories
retrieval hooks and permissions for DNS, DHCP, IPAM, telemetry, ticketing, change, approval, policy, and evidence stores
network owner, DNS owner, DHCP owner, IPAM owner, application owner, security owner, change owner, risk owner, and record owner map
required_outputs: DDI dependency and evidence blocker list
refusal_triggers: required topology, zone, resolver, delegation, DHCP scope, DHCP lease, IPAM prefix, reverse DNS, telemetry, change, or owner evidence is inaccessible
resource owner, service owner, record owner, change owner, risk owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts and missing records
confidence thresholds
source, tenant policy, contractual, privacy, safety, priority, change-authority, owner, or source-of-truth conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend DDI actions safely
source, policy, contractual, privacy, regulated-data, safety, cost, authority, or source-precedence conflict remains unresolved
topology, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, telemetry, incident, change, owner, priority, or approval evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint management, identity architecture, application engineering, firewall/perimeter execution, routing/switching implementation, load-balancing application delivery, broad security architecture, procurement, legal, compliance attestation, budget, staffing, risk acceptance, or production execution
request requires final approval outside the DNS DHCP and IPAM lane
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: DDI demand, planning horizon, requested timing, expected endpoint or workload count, and criticality
available DHCP pool, reserved prefix, resolver, authoritative DNS, change-window, staffing, validation, and rollback capacity
tenant priority, safety, change-freeze, and approval constraints
required_outputs: resource gap and downstream impact summary
refusal_triggers: capacity, utilization, pool, prefix, resolver, authoritative service, change-window, or validation evidence is missing
demand exceeds available capacity and no approved tradeoff authority exists
tenant policy or safety constraint changes the capacity posture without owner confirmation

required_inputs: candidate DNS, DHCP, IPAM, migration, remediation, or audit work items
capacity assessment, risk ranking, priority rule, dependencies, maintenance window, validation gates, and rollback limits
owner and approval context for deferred or high-risk work
refusal_triggers: requested sequence requires unsupported assumptions or unapproved priority rules
recommendation would imply final change, budget, staffing, risk, security, legal, or compliance approval
dependencies or rollback limits are unknown for high-impact DDI changes

required_inputs: approval request text
tenant change, budget, staffing, risk, compliance, legal, safety, and production approval matrix
advisory DDI evidence and recommendation context
refusal_triggers: request asks the specialist to approve a final schedule, budget, staffing plan, production cutover, legal/compliance conclusion, security attestation, risk acceptance, or operational execution
required approver, change owner, business owner, risk owner, or safety authority is missing
tenant policy requires human review before the recommendation can be acted on

required_inputs: cross-industry DDI records with named tenant-industry artifacts
tenant systems of record and sector-specific change, safety, privacy, retention, or availability constraints
authority boundaries for network owner, service owner, change owner, security owner, compliance owner, safety owner, record owner, and orchestrator
refusal_triggers: industry profile is treated cosmetically without named topology, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, change, incident, monitoring, policy, safety, or approval records
request ignores tenant sector, business criticality, change freeze, privacy, retention, safety, or availability facts needed for the lane
tenant record owner, industry-specific constraint, or authority role cannot be determined

notes: Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by issue #1658.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage with validated ability links.
